rules.raw: [-] sudo ** #Use: uv run / npx / task for project commands instead of sudo
rules.hint: Use: uv run / npx / task for project commands instead of sudo

rules.raw: [-] doas ** #Use: uv run / npx / task for project commands instead of doas
rules.hint: Use: uv run / npx / task for project commands instead of doas

rules.raw: [-] dd ** of=* #Destructive disk write with dd is not permitted
rules.hint: Destructive disk write with dd is not permitted

rules.raw: [-] diskutil ** erase ** #Disk erasure is not permitted
[-] ** .. ** #Path traversal is not allowed use cd and direct path access
rules.hint: Path traversal is not allowed use cd and direct path access

rules.raw: [-] killall ** #Use: kill <pid> to target a specific process
rules.hint: Use: kill <pid> to target a specific process

rules.raw: [-] shutdown ** #System power management commands are not permitted
rules.hint: System power management commands are not permitted

rules.raw: [-] reboot ** #System power management commands are not permitted
rules.hint: System power management commands are not permitted

rules.raw: [-] halt ** #System power management commands are not permitted
rules.hint: System power management commands are not permitted

rules.raw: [-] nohup ** #Disallowed: creates unmanaged persistent processes run task in background mode instead
rules.hint: Disallowed: creates unmanaged persistent processes run task in background mode instead

rules.raw: [~] taskkill ** #Killing processes — confirm target and scope
rules.hint: Killing processes — confirm target and scope

rules.raw: [-] python -m ** #Use: uv run python -m <module> to run with the managed environment
rules.hint: Use: uv run python -m <module> to run with the managed environment

rules.raw: [-] python3 -m ** #Use: uv run python -m <module> to run with the managed environment
rules.hint: Use: uv run python -m <module> to run with the managed environment

rules.raw: [-] python ** pip ** #Use: uv pip <command> for package management
rules.hint: Use: uv pip <command> for package management

rules.raw: [-] python3 ** pip ** #Use: uv pip <command> for package management
rules.hint: Use: uv pip <command> for package management

rules.raw: [-] python.exe ** #Use: uv run python to run with the managed environment
rules.hint: Use: uv run python to run with the managed environment

rules.raw: [-] gpip ** #Use: uv pip for package management; uv venv to manage environments
rules.hint: Use: uv pip for package management; uv venv to manage environments

rules.raw: [-] gpip3 ** #Use: uv pip for package management; uv venv to manage environments
rules.hint: Use: uv pip for package management; uv venv to manage environments

rules.raw: [-] gpython ** #Use: uv venv to create or manage Python environments
rules.hint: Use: uv venv to create or manage Python environments

rules.raw: [-] set *=* #Use: VAR=value <command> instead of set — avoids mutating shell state
rules.hint: Use: VAR=value <command> instead of set — avoids mutating shell state

rules.raw: [~] ** > *commands.conf #Writing to commands.conf changes hook policy — confirm this is intentional
rules.hint: Writing to commands.conf changes hook policy — confirm this is intentional

rules.raw: [~] ** >> *commands.conf #Appending to commands.conf changes hook policy — confirm this is intentional
rules.hint: Appending to commands.conf changes hook policy — confirm this is intentional

rules.raw: [-] git ** push ** -f ** #Use: open a PR instead of force-pushing
rules.hint: Use: open a PR instead of force-pushing

rules.raw: [-] git ** push ** --force ** #Use: open a PR instead of force-pushing
rules.hint: Use: open a PR instead of force-pushing

rules.raw: [-] git ** push ** --force-with-lease ** #Use: open a PR instead of force-pushing
rules.hint: Use: open a PR instead of force-pushing

rules.raw: [-] git ** checkout . #Use: git restore <file> or git stash to discard specific changes
rules.hint: Use: git restore <file> or git stash to discard specific changes

rules.raw: [-] git ** restore . #Use: git restore <file> to restore specific files only
rules.hint: Use: git restore <file> to restore specific files only

rules.raw: [-] git ** clean ** -f* ** #Use: git stash or mv <file> ~/.Trash/ to preserve work
rules.hint: Use: git stash or mv <file> ~/.Trash/ to preserve work

rules.raw: [-] git ** add ** -a ** #Use: git add <specific-files> to stage intentionally
rules.hint: Use: git add <specific-files> to stage intentionally

rules.raw: [-] git ** add . #Use: git add <specific-files> to avoid staging secrets or binaries
rules.hint: Use: git add <specific-files> to avoid staging secrets or binaries

rules.raw: [-] git ** reset ** --hard ** #Use: git revert to undo commits without rewriting history
rules.hint: Use: git revert to undo commits without rewriting history

rules.raw: [~] git ** reset ** #Resetting index/HEAD — confirm intent and scope
rules.hint: Resetting index/HEAD — confirm intent and scope

rules.raw: [~] git ** push ** #Confirm: pushing changes to remote
[~] git ** pull ** #Confirm: pulling may overwrite local work
rules.hint: Confirm: pulling may overwrite local work

rules.raw: [~] git ** merge ** #Confirm: merging into current branch
[~] git ** rebase ** #Confirm: rebasing rewrites commit history
[+] git ** checkout ** #Approved: switching branch or restoring files
rules.hint: Approved: switching branch or restoring files

rules.raw: [+] git ** tag ** #Approved: creating or managing tags
[+] git ** add ** #Approved: staging files for commit
[+] git ** rm ** #Approved: removing files from git tracking
rules.hint: Approved: removing files from git tracking

rules.raw: [+] git ** commit ** #Approved: creating a git commit
[~] wget ** #Confirm: wget writes files to disk
rules.hint: Confirm: wget writes files to disk

rules.raw: [+] curl ** #Approved: dangerous variants contained by deny/ask rules above
rules.hint: Approved: dangerous variants contained by deny/ask rules above

rules.raw: [~] tar ** -x* ** #Extracting archive may overwrite existing files
rules.hint: Extracting archive may overwrite existing files

rules.raw: [~] unzip ** #Extracting archive may overwrite existing files
rules.hint: Extracting archive may overwrite existing files

rules.raw: [+] $env:* #Approved: inline environment variable assignment for a single command
rules.hint: Approved: inline environment variable assignment for a single command

rules.raw: [+] $env:* ** #Approved: inline environment variable assignment for a single command
rules.hint: Approved: inline environment variable assignment for a single command

rules.raw: [+] touch ** #Approved: creating or updating file timestamp
rules.hint: Approved: creating or updating file timestamp

rules.raw: [~] uv ** remove ** #Removing package — confirm before modifying dependencies
rules.hint: Removing package — confirm before modifying dependencies

rules.raw: [+] uv ** pip ** #Approved: managing packages via uv pip
rules.hint: Approved: managing packages via uv pip

rules.raw: [-] terraform ** destroy ** #Do not run terraform destroy — ask the user to run this manually
rules.hint: Do not run terraform destroy — ask the user to run this manually

rules.raw: [~] terraform ** apply ** #Confirm: applying Terraform changes to live infrastructure
rules.hint: Confirm: applying Terraform changes to live infrastructure

rules.raw: [+] terraform ** init ** #Approved: initializing Terraform working directory
[~] docker ** push ** #Confirm: pushing image to registry
[+] docker ** run ** #Approved: running a container (valid debug tool)
rules.hint: Approved: running a container (valid debug tool)

rules.raw: [+] docker ** compose ** build ** #Approved: building Docker Compose services
[-] stripe post ** #Stripe API mutations not permitted — use Dashboard instead
rules.hint: Stripe API mutations not permitted — use Dashboard instead

rules.raw: [-] stripe delete ** #Stripe API deletes not permitted — use Dashboard instead
rules.hint: Stripe API deletes not permitted — use Dashboard instead

rules.raw: [-] stripe payouts ** #Payout operations not permitted — use Dashboard instead
rules.hint: Payout operations not permitted — use Dashboard instead

rules.raw: [-] stripe transfers ** #Transfer operations not permitted — use Dashboard instead
rules.hint: Transfer operations not permitted — use Dashboard instead

rules.raw: [-] stripe topups ** #Top-up operations not permitted — use Dashboard instead
rules.hint: Top-up operations not permitted — use Dashboard instead

rules.raw: [+] stripe listen ** #Approved: forwarding webhook events to local endpoint
rules.hint: Approved: forwarding webhook events to local endpoint

rules.raw: [+] stripe trigger ** #Approved: triggering test webhook events
[+] stripe logs ** tail ** #Approved: tailing Stripe API logs
[+] stripe version ** #Approved: Stripe CLI version info
[+] stripe config ** #Approved: viewing Stripe CLI config
[+] stripe status ** #Approved: checking Stripe API status
[+] stripe events ** resend ** #Approved: resending a specific webhook event by ID
rules.hint: Approved: resending a specific webhook event by ID

rules.raw: [~] stripe ** #Confirm: Stripe CLI command — verify intent before running
rules.hint: Confirm: Stripe CLI command — verify intent before running

rules.raw: [-] mkcert ** -install ** #Installing root CA not allowed without manual action
rules.hint: Installing root CA not allowed without manual action

rules.raw: [+] bash ** #Approved: dangerous variants contained by deny/ask rules above
rules.hint: Approved: dangerous variants contained by deny/ask rules above

rules.raw: [+] pwsh ** #Approved: dangerous variants contained by deny/ask rules above
rules.hint: Approved: dangerous variants contained by deny/ask rules above

rules.raw: [-] pwsh ** Remove-Item ** #Deleting files is not permitted
[-] pwsh ** rmdir ** #Removing directories is not permitted
